modules.title: Theory of Measurement
topics: Characteristics of Instruments and measurement systems (Static \& Dynamic)
Error analysis: Sources, types and statistical analysis
DC Voltmeter – Chopper type and solid‑state

modules.title: Cathode Ray Oscilloscope (CRO) and Bridges
topics: Different parts of CRO and block diagram
Special purpose CROs – multi‑input, dual trace, dual beam, sampling, storage (analog and digital)
Schering bridge (high voltage and relative permittivity)

modules.title: Transducers
topics: Displacement transducer – Linear Variable Differential Transformer (LVDT) and Rotary Variable Differential Transformer (RVDT)
Temperature transducers – Resistance Temperature Detector (RTD), Thermistor, Thermocouple
Optical transducers – photo‑emissive, photo‑conductive, photovoltaic, photodiode, phototransistor

modules.title: Signal and Function Generators
topics: Display devices – Light Emitting Diodes (LED) and Liquid Crystal Display (LCD)

modules.title: Digital Instruments and Conversions
topics: Advantages of digital instrument over analog instrument
Digital‑to‑analog conversion (DAC) – variable resistive type, R‑2R ladder, binary ladder, weighted converter using op‑amp and transistor, practical DAC
Analog‑to‑digital conversion (ADC) – ramp technique, dual‑slope integrating type, integrating type (voltage‑to‑frequency), successive approximation
Resolution and sensitivity of digital multimeter

reference_books: Albert D. Helfrick, William David Cooper, “Modern electronic instrumentation and measurement techniques”, TMH 2008.
Oliver Cage, “Electronic Measurements and Instrumentation”, TMH, 2009.
Alan S. Morris, “Measurement and Instrumentation Principles”, Elsevier (Butterworth Heinmann), 2008.
David A. Bell, “Electronic Instrumentation and Measurements”, 2nd Ed., PHI, New Delhi 2008.
H.S. Kalsi, “Electronics Instrumentation”, TMH Ed. 2004.
A.K. Sawhney, “A Course in Electrical and Electronic Measurements and Instrumentation”, Dhanpat Rai.
MMS Anand, “Electronic Instruments & Instrumentation Technology”, PHI Pvt. Ltd., New Delhi Ed. 2005

modules.title: Number Systems
topics: Decimal, Binary, Octal and Hexadecimal systems, conversion from one base to another
Codes – BCD, Excess‑3, Gray, Reflected ASCII, EBCDIC
Logic gates and binary operations – AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, Exclusive‑OR and Exclusive‑NOR
Implementations of logic functions using gates, NAND–NOR implementations, multi‑level gate implementations, multi‑output gate implementations
Boolean postulates and laws – De Morgan’s theorem, principle of duality
Boolean functions, canonical and standard forms, minimization of Boolean functions, minterm, maxterm
Sum of Products (SOP) and Product of Sums (POS)

modules.title: Sequential Logic Design
topics: Building blocks – S‑R, JK and Master‑Slave JK flip‑flops, edge‑triggered flip‑flops
Design of synchronous circuits – Pulse‑train generator

modules.title: Registers and Counters
topics: Synchronous counters – up/down counters, programmable counters
Design of synchronous counters – state diagram, state table, state minimization, state assignment, excitation table and maps, circuit implementation
Registers – shift registers, universal shift registers
Shift‑register counters – ring counter, shift counters

modules.title: Logic Families and Semiconductor Memories
topics: TTL NAND gate – specifications, noise margin, propagation delay, fan‑in, fan‑out, tri‑state operation

experiments: Study of different basic digital logic gates and verification of their truth table.
Study and verification of the law of Boolean algebra and De Morgan’s theorem.
Construction and verification of various combinational circuits such as half adder, full adder, half & full subtractor.
Study of multiplexer and demultiplexer.
Study of different code converters, encoder, and decoder.
Construction and verification of various types of flip‑flops using gates and ICs.
Construction and verification of different shift registers.
Construction and verification of different types of counters.
Study of important TTL technologies and verification of important TTL circuit parameters.
reference_books: Malvino & Leach, “Digital Principles and Applications”, TMH.
M. Morris Mano, “Digital Logic Design”, PHI.
R.P. Jain, “Modern Digital Design”, TMH.
S. Salivahanan & S. Arivazhagan, “Digital Circuits and Design”, Vikas Publishing.
D. Roy Chaudhuri, Digital Circuits, “An Introduction Part ‑1 & 2”, Eureka Publisher.
Ronald J. Tocci, “Digital Systems, Principles and Applications”, PHI.
Taub & Schilling, “Digital Integrated Electronics”, TMH.

modules.title: Semiconductor Material Properties
topics: Bonding forces and energy bands in intrinsic and extrinsic silicon
Special diodes: tunnel diodes, varactor diodes, Schottky diode, photodiodes, photodetector, LED, solar cell

modules.title: Diode Circuits and Power Supply
topics: Power‑supply rectifiers – half‑wave, full‑wave, bridge rectifier
Voltage regulation using shunt regulator circuits
Voltage regulation using series regulator circuits

modules.title: Fundamentals of BJT
topics: Regions of operation: active, cut‑off, saturation
Power dissipation in transistor (Pd, max rating)
Transistor biasing circuits: fixed bias, self bias, voltage‑divider bias, collector‑to‑base bias

modules.title: Small Signal and Large Signal Analysis
topics: Multistage amplifiers: cascading, boot‑strapping, Darlington, cascode
Large‑signal analysis and power amplifiers: Class A, B, AB, C, D, transformer‑coupled, push‑pull

modules.title: FETs, UJT and Thyristors
topics: JFET construction, n‑channel and p‑channel, transfer and drain characteristics, parameters, equivalent model, voltage gain, analysis in CG, CS, CD configurations
Enhancement and depletion MOSFET drain and transfer characteristics
Unijunction transistor (UJT) principle of operation, characteristics, UJT relaxation oscillator

experiments: 1. Diode Characteristic – pn junction diode characteristics and static & dynamic resistance measurement from graph; plot Zener diode characteristic curve.
2. Clipper & Clamper – plot the characteristic curves of various clipper circuits; plot the characteristic curves of various clamper circuits.
3. Half‑wave, Full‑wave & Bridge Rectifier – measure Vrms and Vdc for half‑wave, full‑wave and bridge rectifier; measure ripple factor and rectification ratio for full‑wave and half‑wave rectifiers.
4. Voltage Regulation – voltage regulation using Zener‑diode shunt regulator and transistor series voltage regulator under varying input and varying load conditions.
5. BJT Characteristics – plot input & output characteristic curves in CB & CE configurations; determine α, β and Q‑point from the curves; plot characteristic curves of various clipper circuits.
6. h‑Parameter – measure h‑parameters (Av, Ai, Ro, Ri) in a CE amplifier.
7. Multi‑Stage Amplifier – plot characteristic curves for direct‑coupled, RC‑coupled, and transformer‑coupled amplifiers.
8. FET Characteristics – plot characteristic curve for n‑channel JFET in CS configuration; determine pinch‑off voltage from the curve.
9. UJT Characteristic – plot characteristic curve for UJT; determine intrinsic stand‑off ratio.
reference_books: Millman & Halkias, “Electronic Devices And Circuits”, TMH.
Salivahanan, Kumar & Vallavaraj, “Electronic Devices And Circuits”, TMH.
Boylestad & Neshelsky, “Electronic Devices & Circuits”, PHI.
Schilling & Belove, “Electronic Circuits, Discrete & Integrated”, TMH.
Chattopadhyay & Rakhshit, “Electronic Fundamentals & Applications”, New Age.
Adel S. Sedra & Kenneth C. Smith, “Microelectronic Circuits”, OUP.
R. A. Gayakwad, “Op‑Amps And Linear Integrated Circuits”, PHI.
Theodore F. Bogart, Jeffrey S. Beasley, “Guillermo Rico Electronic Devices & Circuits”.
Allen Mottershead, “Electronic Devices & Circuits”.

modules.title: Introduction to circuit theory
topics: Basic circuit elements R, L, C and their characteristics (linearity and time‑dependence)
Voltage and current sources (controlled and uncontrolled)
Kirchhoff’s Current Law (KCL) and Kirchhoff’s Voltage Law (KVL) analysis
Concept of phasor and vector representation

modules.title: Network Theorems
topics: Problems involving dependent and independent sources

modules.title: Transient analysis and Laplace transform
topics: Transients in RL, RC and RLC circuits
Laplace transform solution of integro‑differential equations
Transformation of waveforms (step, ramp, gate, sinusoidal functions)
Application of network theorems in the transform domain

modules.title: Two‑port parameters
topics: Z, Y, ABCD and Hybrid parameters, including inverse and image parameters
Reciprocity and symmetry in all parameters

experiments: To Verify Thevenin Theorem and Superposition Theorem.
To Verify Reciprocity Theorem and Millman’s Theorem.
To Verify Maximum Power Transfer Theorem.
To Determine Open Circuit and Short Circuit parameters of a Two Port Network.
To Determine A, B, C, D parameters of a Two Port Network.
To determine h parameters of a Two Port Network.
To Find Frequency Response of RLC Series Circuit and RLC Parallel Circuit and determine resonance and 3 dB frequencies.
To determine charging and discharging times of Capacitors.
reference_books: M.E. Van Valkenburg, Network Analysis (Pearson)
S. P. Ghosh, A. K. Chakraborty, Network Analysis & Synthesis (MGH)
http://www.nptelvideos.in/2012/11/networks-and-systems.html
Sudhakar, Circuit Network Analysis & Synthesis (TMH)
J. David Irwin, Engineering Circuit Analysis, 10th edition (Wiley India)
Kuo, Network Analysis & Synthesis (Wiley India)
Robert L. Boylestad, Introductory Circuit Analysis (Pearson)
Smarajit Ghosh, NETWORK THEORY: ANALYSIS AND SYNTHESIS (PHI)
Roy Choudhary D., Network and Systems (New Age Publications)
Bhattacharya and Singh, Network Analysis & Synthesis (Pearson)

experiments: Study of Cathode Ray Oscilloscope.
Study of displacement measurement by LVDT.
Force measurement by strain gauge.
Measurement of Capacitor using Q-meter.
Measurement of Self-induction using Q-meter.
Temperature measurement by thermistor.
Study of optical Transducers: Photo-diode, Photo-Transistor.
Design of digital to analog converter, R-2R ladder Type and analysis of its characteristics.
Measurement of the unknown Inductance by using Maxwell’s bridge method.
Measurement of the unknown capacitance by using Schering bridge method.
Measurement of the unknown Frequency by using Wein’s bridge method.
Measurement of the unknown Inductance by using Hay’s bridge method.
Calculate Frequency and amplitude using CRO & Function Generator.
Calculate Frequency using Lissajous Pattern.
Study RVDT.
Study of Function Generator.
Temperature measurement by thermocouple.
Temperature measurement by RTD.
Study of optical Transducers: Photo conductive, Photo voltaic.
Study digital Multimeter.
